preamble: The voiceless postalveolar fricative /ʃ/ can undergo a phonetic transformation to the voiceless alveolo-palatal fricative /ɕ/, a shift often associated with palatalization and delabialization. This change involves a fronting of the articulation from the postalveolar region towards the alveolo-palatal area, often accompanied by a reduction in lip rounding.

examples.note: The word 'fish' in Luxembourgish, where the historical /ʃ/ has merged with /ç/ to become /ɕ/ in younger speakers.

sources: Conrad, F. (2021). Regional differences in the evolution of the merger of /ʃ/ and /ç/ in Luxembourgish. Journal of the International Phonetic Association.
Hall, T. A. (2013). The phonology of German. Oxford University Press.
